question.text: Under Section 11(4) of the SEBI Act, SEBI can attach bank accounts or property of an intermediary involved in violations for a maximum period of:

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Section 11(4) of the SEBI Act empowers SEBI to attach bank accounts or other property of any intermediary or person associated with the securities market involved in violation for up to 90 days. **The Trap:** Candidates may confuse this with other time limits in securities law, such as the 45-day appeal period for SAT or 60-day appeal period for Supreme Court. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series III-B (Issuers Compliance) also covers SEBI's enforcement powers. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The 90-day attachment period is a temporary measure to prevent dissipation of assets during investigation.
